channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: University of Minnesota leaders say ICE has detained a graduate student
item.description: The University of Minnesota says one of its graduate students is being detained by U.S. immigration authorities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wisconsin AG sues to stop Musk's check handout
item.description: Wisconsin Attorney General Josh Kaul sued Elon Musk on Friday over his proposal to pay two people $1 million for voting in the state's Supreme Court race. “The Wisconsin Department of Justice is committed to ensuring that elections in Wisconsin are safe, secure, free, and fair.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, have received email notices of their mass firing — the latest step in the Trump administration’s government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top vaccine official allegedly pushed out of FDA: report
item.description: Dr. Peter Marks, who played a key role in Operation Warp Speed, was reportedly forced out of the FDA, according to the Wall Street Journal. In his purported resignation letter, Marks slams HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High-profile ICE arrests of Tufts, Alabama students spotlight growing Trump sweep
item.description: Headline-grabbing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) arrests this week at Tufts University and the University of Alabama show growing momentum behind the Trump administration's battle against pro-Palestinian foreign students and faculty.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Republican lawmaker confronted over Signal chat leak, responds sharply
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn., had a harsh response to a man asking him about his trust in the Trump team after the Signal chat leak. The congressman's reply seemed to stop the man in his tracks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump-blocking judges managed to get past Senate judiciary hawks
item.description: There is some disagreement among experts and Republicans over whether enough was done to block Democratic-nominated judges, particularly those who are now blocking the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump draws laughs when defining a 'woman' — until he touches on a serious issue
item.description: When asked to define a "woman," President Donald Trump started with funny lines before turning to a serious issue, the inclusion of transgender athletes in women's sports, which he called "crazy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates former Arizona attorney general for US ambassador to Serbia
item.description: President Donald Trump has nominated former Arizona Attorney General Mark Brnovich to serve in his administration as the next U.S. ambassador to Serbia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major law firm reaches deal with Trump to avoid White House order even as two other firms sue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent international law firm reached a deal with President Donald Trump on Friday to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services and to review its hiring practices, averting a punishing executive order like the ones directed at nearly a half-dozen other major legal
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump admin’s first oil and gas-lease sales nets nearly $40 million for U.S. coffers
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum lauded millions in federal windfall from sales of oil and gas leases in Trump's first three months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Republicans say Sigal Chattah nominated for Nevada U.S. Attorney post
item.description: Nevada Republicans are saying Sigal Chattah has been nominated to serve as interim U.S. Attorney overseeing Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump endorses budget fix that would restore Washington, DC, shortfall and urges House to pass it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is pushing the House of Representatives to pass a measure that would prevent immediate financial disaster for the District of Columbia even as he continues to level harsh criticism at the city and its leaders.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Noted economist honored by Trump warns his 25% tariffs could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns in a new analysis that President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on auto imports could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle and says the proposed taxes could weaken the ability of U.S. automakers to compete with their foreign counterparts.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Fewer Americans now see Canada as a close US ally as Trump strains a longtime partnership
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans are less likely to see Canada and the U.S. as close allies than they were two years ago, the latest indication that President Donald Trump's tariff threats and talk of taking over a neighboring ally are souring a critical economic and military relationship. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DFLers at Minnesota Capitol rip Musk’s ideas while the GOP wants a closer look
item.description: Billionaire and top Trump adviser Elon Musk is reshaping the federal government. In Minnesota, Democrats are increasingly turning Musk mentions into a punching bag. And Republicans say his work could be replicated in St. Paul.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump team revokes $11 billion in funding for addiction, mental health care
item.description: The Trump administration says it hopes to save $11.4 billion by freezing and revoking COVID-era grants. Addiction experts say clawing back the federal funding is risky and could put patients at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Foragers want a seat at the rulemaking table as DNR mulls mushroom, berry bag limits
item.description: The DNR is considering bag limits on how much food people can take from state-managed lands. That gallon limit is ringing alarm bells for foraging groups like the Minnesota Mycological Society.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11
